choices: A) To make code run faster
B) To transfer control of object creation from application code to a framework/container
C) To invert the order of method calls
D) To reverse the class hierarchy
gradedQuestions.prompt: What is the primary purpose of Inversion of Control (IoC) in Spring?
In Spring, what is the name of the metadata object that describes a bean before it is instantiated?
Which annotation marks a class as a source of bean definitions in Spring?
What is the default scope of a Spring bean?

expectedPoints: BeanDefinition allows the container to know about beans before creating them
Enables lazy initialization, scope management, and dependency resolution
feedbackHints: Think about what the container needs to know before it can create a bean
reflectionQuestions.prompt: Why does Spring separate bean metadata (BeanDefinition) from actual bean instances?
rubric: Mentions separation of definition from instantiation
Mentions lazy creation or lifecycle control
